### Post by neonl on 2007-11-27
> **Rui Pais said:**
> One nice trick to control start up apps is to make a script, make it executable and then create a .desktop for it like described above. That way you just run one startup "app" that can launch what ever you want and how you want and editable with any text editor. Here an example (start oofice quick launcher and numlockX):
```
#!/bin/sh
# <- this symbol makes the line just a comment, remove it to launch the app:
        # gnome-settings-daemon &
        ooffice -quickstart -nologo -nodefault &
        numlockx on &
        # gnome-volume-manager &
```
Controlling the look of Applications is another aspect that it's not obvious. 
E17 Apps use controls/widgets from etk, but gtk apps don't know nothing about it. That means they will have the look (kind of ugly) of default gnome.
There are 2 ways of tweak this. 
1st way, if you have gnome or xfce installed, is simply run the daemons of those environment, gnome-settings-daemon or xfce-mcs-manager respectively, on startup (like described above). That will set the theme we choose for gnome/xfce for all gtk apps, runs some basic services and start a few things like screensaver and numlockX. Appearence can then be tuned by running gnome-control-center or xfce-setting-show, according the daemon used.

Rui, I always used the "second" method of tweaking the GTK look, but now I wanted to try this one. I don't understand exactly what I have to do. Is it create a text file doing```
nano ~/.desktop
```and then writing```
#!/bin/sh
# <- this symbol makes the line just a comment, remove it to launch the app:
        # gnome-settings-daemon &
        ooffice -quickstart -nologo -nodefault &
        numlockx on &
        # gnome-volume-manager &
```saving and quiting, and then doing```
sudo chmod +x ~/.desktop
```?

EDIT: this one is solved, ovious solution I had to create a launcher and then select it to run @ startup.

Regards, Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-11-27
> **neonl said:**
> 
EDIT: can somebody explain me what exactly is Oz OS and what users is it destinate to serve, etc?

> **smartboyathome said:**
> OzOS is basically a distro which will use E17 in a different manor by installing E17 from CVS and keeping it up to date through CVS.

> **neonl said:**
> Yeah, I knew that part... I was particular interested in when it's going to be released is it for home usage, will it use any GTK or will it be ETK exclusively...

Yes, [Oz OS]("http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php/topic,907.0.html") will be centered on e17 compiled from cvs as main desktop and would use its metapackages to manage Ubuntu packages tunning for use with e17. 

The main ideas is to be dynamic and flexible. 
It will allow compile of e17 from cvs at anytime user wants, it will have a small iso, because it will only contains metapackages (that will access Ubuntu repos or Oz for tunned special ones). 
And besides trying to offer a nice balanced set of apps to work with e17 (in 3 "flavors" of different levels of features), will take the use of metapackages  a step further by offering at installation sets of "profile" for several typical uses (scientific, graphical/art work, multimedia, education/kids, etc.)
It will have the preferences of the users since installation, and pretends to make they life easier from the very beginning.
So it's target to anyone, except maybe users without Internet (but maybe we end up making extra isos with the debs needed...)


neonl, have you solved the issue of bad shelves with a new set of confs? or it's related with brakeage that smartboyathome mention?


> **smartboyathome said:**
> Just to let everyone know, the shelfs broke today, so don't try going anything "weird" ;)

Thanks for the warning!
I will check it for when it will back to normality.
Can you give more details? what happened to shelfs?

Thanks, Rui.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-11-28
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Rui, I can't seem to compile emotion, extrackt, and eclair. I got all the dependancies resolved (and took them off the skip list in ~/.easy_e17.conf), but the script just skips them, even when I put sudo easy_e17.sh --only=emotion,extrackt,eclair. Help please!

Hi smartboyathome

easy_e17.sh, from deb version use it's own ,easy_e17.conf at /etc/.easy_e17.conf. You need to change that one.

hth.


**@all,**
i am testing a new update, that have a workaround for the ewl issue. So users don't need to care with repeating 2 or 3 times the command.

For the more purest that may be worried with taht workaround, thats nothing to do with compilation. Compile always finish fine. 
The issue it's at install process. It use the already available edje (edje_cc) to create ewl theme and propagate it to default path. 
Thats where it fails... apparently only a certain maximum of stuff can be "imported" and is been exceeded. Later (maybe ewl theme is growing in size) it needs 3 runs to complete install/propagate ewl data to /etc/opt.

I will move too the included theme to an extra optional package. 
e17-cvs will have only that, e from cvs. 

I'll just go back go my tests now.

Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-01
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Awesome! Awaiting the update. :)

Ok it's done.

Just write a file /etc/easy_e17-custom.conf with your --skip list (and any other option and run apt-get install e17-cvs. 

To go back to default, just remove or move away your custom file and run again apt-get install e17-cvs. :)

Just as a side note, as long as you have e17 already install, apt-get install e17-cvs only do an update and will not reinstall all.

Please let me know if you hit any problems.
Rui

---

### Post by smartboyathome on 2007-12-01
Would a symlink from /etc/easy_e17-custom.conf to my ~/.easy_e17.conf file work? Also, would there be a way to have the package do that symlink automatically so people don't have to be root to edit it?

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-01
Hi,
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Would a symlink from /etc/easy_e17-custom.conf to my ~/.easy_e17.conf file work?

No, i tried here and it needs to be a really file symlinks seems to generate an empty .easy_e17.conf file.

> **smartboyathome said:**
> Also, would there be a way to have the package do that symlink automatically so people don't have to be root to edit it?

My first idea was to do exactly that... but the point it's exactly not have a file. A user that want to change default options should create his/her own file (at his/her own's risk). 
This should be done only by experimented users! 

My first idea was more or less the same that yours, but i want to keep things relatively safe and if that was implemented automatically, with the multitude of .conf files that may be already exist on e17 users of this method... the results would be very hard to predict.  

That way, it stills very flexible, but requires user to make it's own file and use root powers. If somethings goes wrong, user knows what the problem is and can delete or check it's own file.

It's not working 100%, i need to implement default conf files in different way, so remove would in fact act if file changes (a deb specification issue that i forget). 
It's done but i can't upload right now, because Cafelinux is raising it's security levels and I'm waiting for an extra password for make it visible to outside. 
I let you know when it's available.

Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-03
Hi, RM,
> **neonl said:**
> So there isn't nothing that we (by we I mean the common user) can do to make it better?
well that i don't know... i wish an answer to that too... it's a little annoying and ugly (i had an idea that it starts to happen only a month ago or so) 

> **neonl said:**
> Rui, I have one doubt: what can I do to use the PrintScreen key to do screenshots without having the screenshot module inserted into a shelf?

You can tune that from:
Configuration Panel -> Keyboard & Mouse -> Key Bindings

If it won't list key 'Print', press add key and follow instructions that appear on screen (tip: use Launch: Definied Command for simplicity).

You can set it to use scrot (use scrot -h for options) or another app from other DE. I use gnome screenshot that i like a lot.[ATTACH]52180[/ATTACH]
:)

Your problem can require a dirty trick but maybe it can be solved easily and without such things.
Lets try different ways, starting from the safest, till we get that issue solved.

First, do you download dome deb package or used CaféLinux repos?
Retry to install to see if it finishes now.
If you install from repos just do:
```
sudo aptitude reinstall e17-cvs 
```
If you use a deb package do:
```
sudo dpkg -i e17-cvs_*number_of_version_i_have*.deb
```
replacing the *number_of_version_i_have* by the one you have.

*EDIT* please avoid mix version numbers. try to reinstall the same package that it's broken.

Post how it runs.
Another suggestion, if that fails, 
Run synaptic and go 'Custom Filter' -> Broken and if e17-cvs is visible in that section double click on it (some times synaptic solve those issues better)

If that fails i'll tell you what remove manually and what files edit (the dangerous ones...)

Good luck
Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-06
> **neonl said:**
> What about updating? Is it giving problems too, or is it just installing?


Ok, i finally manage to update.
Just need to remove previously evas compilation:
```
sudo rm -rf ~/.e17_cvs/e17/libs/evas/*
```
and then a normal: 
```
sudo easy_e17.sh
```

hth

Ok vamos com calma então.

The 1st thing is comment/delete that repo from your sources.list. 
Don't mix repos of e17. It's the 1st ticked to get deep  troubles and misterious bugs. Some repos install libs on /usr/libs oothers on /usr/local/libs, others on /opt/e17/libs soon they start to mix each other with different versions... No. avoid that like the plague!

Are you sure that the broken version is e17-cvs? (note that the name is e17-cvs, not e17 or enlightenment)
If thats the case the version should be 0.0-4.1.
I would advise to download it again [from here]("http://ruialeixopais.planetaclix.pt/linux/e17/e17-cvs_0.0-4.1_all.deb").  and run:
```
sudo dpkg -i e17-cvs_0.0-4.1_all.deb
```
if code stops at ewl package repeat the above command 2 or 3 times till it continues (that version may not contain a later workaround for that issue).

lets see if that solves.
If not we clean all by hand.

Boa sorte 
(Então é(s) brazileiro? Já me tinha perguntado se seria deste ou desse lado do oceano, ou apenas um conhecedor de umas palavas ocasionais ;) )

Olá de novo Fred.

No problem, the repos on the 1st post are fully compatible with your actual installation. 
When i say mixing repos, means add other then those repos when e17 is installed by these method. 

It will not reinstall all. It will only update the script version and recompile any e17 app, lib or module that meanwhile had any update on cvs server.
It will make updates and any uninstall of e17-cvs much easier :)

> 
Anyway ... obrigado por tudo!

Abracos

Fred

De nada,

Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-09

Oops, i had answer an identical question on another thread and almost forget this issue here...

The issue was the format
```
font: "Sans:style=Bold,Edje-Vera-Bold"
```
on themes (old font: "Edje-Vera-Bold" worked) and other issues related with fonts (disappearing legend on some modules and conf windows).

The problem was some new way, i think, of dealing with system fonts that required a new dependency libfontconfig1-dev and a recompilation of some e libs against it.
:)

Hi Rui, sorry this late answer (busy all day...)

You must by know find the answer, but i posted to help others on config e17 modules.

It's simple. Right click on desired module and choose 'Able to be resized', then right click again and choose 'Begin Move/Resize this gadget'. Thats all :)

### Post by Scruffynerf on 2007-12-11
> **Rui Pais said:**
> Thats strange, it use to install normally on feisty (even on edgy...)
Do you have any special repos enabled that overlap ubuntu ones (automatix or other like that)?

I will check and see if i can reproduce this and let you know.

Not automatix, however I do have a bunch of custom repo's.

```

## Moblock Repo
# deb http://moblock-deb.sourceforge.net/debian feisty main
# deb-src http://moblock-deb.sourceforge.net/debian feisty main

## Ubuntu Ultimate Repo
# deb http://repoubuntusoftware.info/ feisty all

## Repo purely for Thunderbird 2
# deb http://ubuntu.iuculano.it feisty thunderbird
# deb-src http://ubuntu.iuculano.it feisty thunderbird

#Innotek repos or VirtualBox
# deb http://www.virtualbox.org/debian feisty non-free

deb http://cafelinux.org/Downloads/oz-os tinwoodman main contrib
```

To expound:
Moblock is purely for the Moblock IP filter.
Virtualbox is purely for the Virtualbox virtualisation software. I find it easier to use than VMWare, which often segfaults on my system.
Cafelinux is yours
Ultimate is the repo for the customised distribution formerly known as Ubuntu Ultimate, a project led by the fellow here known as TheeMan. I've used it exclusively for games.

I have also in the past used medibuntu for a few things, however for some reason that I forget, I've removed from my current sources list.

Again, I have also used Trevhino's repo, mainly from an attempt to get compiz working (it failed)

### Post by Scruffynerf on 2007-12-11
I doubt that you will be able to reproduce it.

Delving into the dependencies (no wonder they called it hell), the "libsqlite3-0" package is not removable - removing it will remove 142+ applications, including such gems as gconf.... The "libpam0g" package does the same.

So it looks like a problem with my particular installation / combination of software packages.

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-11
> **Scruffynerf said:**
> I doubt that you will be able to reproduce it.

Delving into the dependencies (no wonder they called it hell), the "libsqlite3-0" package is not removable - removing it will remove 142+ applications, including such gems as gconf.... The "libpam0g" package does the same.

So it looks like a problem with my particular installation / combination of software packages.

well the repos on your previous post are commented, so they shouldn't be in use. But you may have used and one of them could changed those packages... maybe if you enable them again they have a limpam0-dev compatible... dont know.

You can try to use synaptic or dpkg to force to go to a specific version and after that try to  solve eventual breakage of dependent package... but thats a situation with unpredictable results. I would not advise to do that.

libpam may be avoided (it will only prevent you lock screen from e17)
try to follow the manual procedure, [described here]("http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php?action=dlattach;topic=881.0;attach=2449") and remove libpam0-dev from the list of aptitude install on step 1 of the how-to.

Post on any doubt.
Rui

### Post by FishFilet on 2007-12-16
I am having the same problem as this user.

> **flatwombat said:**
> Great tutorial!  E-17 is working properly on a new Gutsy install except for changing the screen resolution.  The pop-up response is:

"Your X Display Server is missing support for Xrander (X Resize and Rotate) extension.  You cannot change the screen resolutions without the support of  this extension.  It could also be possible that at the time ecore was built, there was no Xrandr support detected."

Of course, Xrandr is installed and confirmed by apt as the latest version.  Just wondering if there's a quick fix?  Thanks.

I also used a work around posted by the same user.

> **flatwombat said:**
> Thanks for the response, Rui Pais, but I was able to change the resolution perfectly in Gnome, which is why it was a bit confusing.  Edit: Using a workaround.  Edited /etc/X11/xorg.conf and removed the resolutions higher than 1024x768 (after backing up the original xorg.conf). about the xorgconf, thats not a workaround. It's a fix of a bad config. Regrettably graphic cards/monitor detection and xorg auto configuration still have a lot of issues.

On VPN, can you tell me how or what do you on gnome to get VPN? (i never used VPN)

About the link you point, it's a very old how-to... if you have experience with both environment you can use it and adapted for any new stuff, other wise i would advise to avoid it (EDIT: AND if you decide by give a try YOU MUST DELETE ALL FROM THIS METHOD. Don't mix this with pre-compiled stuff from other repos or you get troubles). If you want a mix of those environment i would advise you to give an eye to Geubuntu, that may offer more or less the same, updated, and with less trouble. 
But i don't know if that would solve your specific issues...

I've searched thru the thread and several other places both in and out of the forums but my limited knowledge hasn't yielded any solutions to me so I decided to start over and ask for help.

I've also read thru the pdf of the original instructions and didn't spot anything helpful to my situation.

I now have a clean command line install with only xorg and xdm added and no configuration done yet.

It does appear that most people posting in this thread already had a complete GUI system installed and simply added E17 to it so that could be where I'm missing a step.

I realize my problem may not be related directly to E17 so if someone could direct me to something I missed in another thread, etc. I'd appreciate it.

Thanks in advance.

Hi RRS, 
yes all this method presumes that you have an Login Manager (like GDM/KDM) i'm not much experienced with plain XDM. 
You may need to edit some conf file... but first try to create a file:
 **~/.xsession**
with one line:
```
exec /opt/e17/bin/enlightenment_start
```
And then link it with:
```
ln -s ~/.xsession ~/.xinitrc
```
That should your startx work with e17.

Post your results since they may be useful for other users too.
Thanks, 
Rui

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-17

Glad it's working!  :)
Hope you got all dependencies solved by now. I would suspect more of Ultimate repo, but didn't check myself (browse the repos on-line should give the answer...)


**@all**
I updated the repos last night.
Last version 0.0-4.4.2 highlights:
- Much better PATH dealing
- Remove of old hacks relate with PATH (should have done this a long time ago...)
- Avoid aborted installation on minimal installs where some paths related with default sessions may be missing.
- Blocks installation on Geubuntu installs (Geubuntu will block this method, too. Cooperation it's good!)
- User can install only scripts and confs, skipping compilation by previously creating an empty file ''~/_skip_easy_e17_install"
- e17-themes is lighter now. 
- I made an e17-themes-extra for my tunes ctHulhain and eventually others that may be made on Oz context)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-17
> **FishFilet said:**
> ...

Then I log out and log into E17 and I can get on the internet.

This is just one example of things another one would be VPN which i use frequently but at any rate can anyone point me in the right direction?

Hi again,
about wireless.
The enlightenment app that deals with wireless to e17  it's exalt.
It's not in the main cvs. It seems to be a little buggy, and i've been told that it has issues with preserving passwords fro connections...

Another app that people seems to prefer it's the generic Wicd.
Check here for this subject and a deb package:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=473331](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=473331)

About exalt, if you want to try it, you can get it here:
[http://watchwolf.fr/wiki/doku.php?id=exalt](http://watchwolf.fr/wiki/doku.php?id=exalt).
In order to compile you need to add to your .bashrc:
```
export PKG_CONFIG_PATH=/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig
```
to compile (check order, it's important).
I made packages compatible with e17-cvs, but exalt module always crash when i tried to access configurations...

---

### Post by smartboyathome on 2007-12-17
By the way Rui, I use WICD with E17 and found it integrates nicely. Just add the net module, right click it > configuration, and set Launch Application On Double-Click to /opt/wicd/gui.py. This will pull WICD's GUI up for you. I would say that WICD should be packaged with Oz as well. ;)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-18
> **FishFilet said:**

Well as long as you like or are used to an app, i see no reson for stop using it (i'm completly against certain positions for pure DEs from fundamentalist users, and in relation to e17 even more since most of it's apps are very young, incomplete and buggy). 
If apps don't have an entry on menus you can make one like smartboyathome said before. See mt 1st post too for some tips on create app launchers/menu entries. (The one you mention it's called users-admin).

> **FishFilet said:**
> 
Another thing that is bugging me is that many icons dont have any ... well icons i guess. It is just the name of the item and no Icon. How can I fix this?
Same thing as above. Run the app from menu right click on default icon and choose Edit icon set what you like.

> **FishFilet said:**
> 
I have heard that E alone is not enough to have. I guess because it is a WM and not a DE. But it seems to me that you should be able to do just about anything. Am I wrong here? Will I be missing some special functionality that I will need if I am not using a DE (Gnome)?
No e it's not a pure WM nor a full DE (something in between), but the reason that it don't have all functionality it's because no one care yet to make it or the ones available are too buggy to be used (i cut a lot of them with this method because of that). 
But again nothing should prevent you from using tools from independent sources or from other DEs. This is Linux and all it's GPL or at least free of use. :)

> **FishFilet said:**
> 
I would Like to have a bar at the botom of the screen with my programs in it that functions like the apple OS bar where when I hover over the icons they get bigger and mover around and such. I saw this on an E demo on you tube I think How can I get that effect.

That it's get with ibar module.
The specific animation you refer it's theme related. It's implemented on some thmes. Intall the ones on [www.get-e.org](www.get-e.org) and see the one that gives what you like. 
You can use the Advanced properties of theme selector (Configuration panel > Appearence > Themes > Advanced) that allow to set a theme for all e17 and another one for module/ibar
(or any other e17 element, e17 it's highly flexible :))

> **FishFilet said:**
> 
and last but not least, Thank You so much for all the help to everyone here at the forums. It is the best forum I have ever been on. The people are very helpful and response times fast. I could not leave Vista without you all!

No problem :)
I'm glad more people are starting to use enlightenment and the myth of an hard or unstable environment it's slowly fade away.

Have fun
Rui

### Post by smartboyathome on 2007-12-19
You can launch it in the run box (type in the name of the program and you should get it), and then open the run box again and this time push the up arrow and you should get a list of recently used commands (with the most recent, i.e. your program, at the bottom).

Hi smartboyathome,
you mean ibox? Yes thats what a call a nice feature (:lol:) it's just the way e do it. There is no plan to implement Gnome features or way of doing things... 
For me, i always hated the gey panels wasting space of gnome/kde/windows that get full of strange boxs replicating the name of open windows (that i'm already look at...) 
I prefer a clean look. I always set ibox transparent on a shelf and that way it only became "apparent" when i minimize something. I use a big pager (pager are so cool and discrete on e17) where apps appeara represented by it's size and icon that i used to change apps/desktop...

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-20
> **FishFilet said:**
> How can I know what the name of a particular app is, or more importantly the command to launch it in the terminal. To a windows user it seems you all just know, lol. Is there a similar method?

Another way to know the name of binaries (besides the one smartboyathome explained) it's either check how the launcher on gnome menus implemented or open synaptic and check what each app have saved on /usr/bin/ using the 'Installed Files' tab of properties.


BTW, yet another way of tunning icons on menus (on a previous of yours) it's:
Configuration Panel > Appearance > Icon Themes

It allow to change menu icons according to icon themes (gtk) you may have installed. Some are more complete then others (or may fit e look better)

Rui

### Post by RRS on 2007-12-22
Was busier then I thought I'd be this last week but finally got a chance to try your suggestions from post 434.

Unfortunately creating the file and link made no difference so I started rechecking my notes and steps again.

Since the xsession errors made no mention of e17 or xdm I decided to reinstall xorg, even though the originall installation and configuration steps had appeared to go ok.

I'd originally used:```
sudo apt-get install xserver-xorg-video-i810
```to avoid all the uneeded drivers.

To reinstall I used:```
sudo aptitude install xorg
```
Expecting to see all the missing drivers along with whatever else may not have been installed the first time, I was surprised that only a few additional items were installed. Among them was the xfonts packages so that apparently was my problem from the beginning!

This time "startx" brought up a fully functional Enlightenment desktop :)
Still gotta install some programs though to turn "functional" into "usefull" :)

A reboot brought up the XDM login screen which to then took me straight to the desktop so that all works as well too now.

I don't know if the xsession file and link you suggested are still needed but I plan to leave them just in case.

BTW, the reason I installed this way was to re-invigorate an old Dell.
900MH Celeron, 192 MB of ram, and a 10G hard drive.

Thanks again.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2007-12-24
> **TeaSwigger said:**
> Pardon my newbie-ness to things cvs, but I tried installing via the repository (via Synaptic) and the install script hangs dpkg - for over a day now - on the following little error:
```
cvs [update aborted]: connect to anoncvs.enlightenment.org(140.211.167.167):2401 failed: Connection timed out.
```
:(

How can I either fix this or get it to quit attempting the install and undo whatever's been done so I can make dpgk happy again?

Hi, 
that seems indeed a cvs server failure. :( 


> **TeaSwigger said:**
> This does raise a question. If I may ask, how may someone stop an install in progress without dpkg "hanging up" on that job? Using this e17 installation for example. If dpkg is still waiting in this script for e17 from cvs, how could you "kill" it and move on to install something else instead? Say you use dial up and your connection bites the dust half way in, the job just isn't going to finish, so you'd rather cancel this transfer and installation of e17 and get xfce from a xubuntu CD.

Sorry about that dpkg issue.In order to get your dpkg back to order you need to fish e17-cvs first. You can remove it later if you don't like it. 
When dpkg hangs on a package generally it needs to finish that installation in order to go back to normality. 
You can try to do an *sudo apt-get install -f* and see if that fix it, but i doubt it that it will work if script was already running...

You can make it faster by doing:
```
touch _skip_easy_e17_install
```
before:
```
apt-get install e17_cvs
```

After finish dpkg correctly you can then finish the installation;
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -i
```
or remove/clean all you your don't want it:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -c -c -c 
sudo apt-get remove --purge e17-cvs 
```

---

apparently a bunch of packages (such as the kde4 related ones) depended on libungif4-dev.  but a lot of packages (again specifically the kde4 packages i'm aware of) are changing dependencies away from libungif4-dev to libgif4.  but not all of the packages have been updated yet to reflect this change.  so i get:
```
e17-cvs:
 Depends: libungif4-dev but it is not going to be installed
```i don't suppose there is any way to force e17-cvs to depend on libgif4-dev instead of libungif4-dev?

Hi.
Sorry i didn't get time yet to try Hardy... 

I will try to see if that can be done, doing libgif4-dev and libungif4-dev according to need version...

Meanwhile, my only suggestion would be doing the old manual way (this deb it's just a wrapper for that anyway...)
Just make the replace you want on 1st step of the procedure.

Check the pdf version here, for a detailed how-to:
[http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php/topic,881.0.html](http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php/topic,881.0.html)

Hope that help.

### Post by disturbedite on 2008-01-05
sorry for the double post, but i have an update/clarification about the issue on hardy...
apparently libgif4 & libungif4 conflict.  as i said, a lot of kde4 packages used to depend on libungif4 but they are transitioning the kde4 packages (for kubuntu at least) to depend on libgif4 instead...
so that means having packages installed that depend on libgif4 will prevent one from being able to install e17 cuz of e17's dependency on libungif4-dev.

 ```

> **whayong said:**

Hi,
yes the install process it's suppose to skip several apps, but not the ones you mention (eet, ecore...) 
Did it skip them when install or only when update?
Can you login to enlightenment?

The reason why there are stuff skipped it's because they are not much stable or maintainned anymore. 
But you can still install them anyway... Check the 1st post for instructions on how to do it...

### Post by whayong on 2008-01-07
Yes, I was able to log into enlightenment.  Infact, I've been using it for about a day now.  I can't remember now if those apps/libs I mentioned were skipped during the initial install.  I can tell you though that I don't have any of the enlightenment apps (picture viewer, media player, etc...).  A few more questions:

Has anyone got suspend/hibernate working on a laptop when the lid is closed?  It works when clicking the suspend/hibernate options but not when closing the lid.  I guess it's something I could live with.

Does anyone have any suggestions for firefox themes?  I guess it makes sense that GNOME apps aren't affected by the selected e17 theme.

How does one change the entrance theme?  There doesn't seem to be an option under configuration.

WICD works well with this by the way.

EDIT: I just re read the original post regarding gtk themes at startup.  This solved the whole firefox thing.  Is it possible to get suspend when laptop lid is closed the same way?  I'll give it a shot.

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-07
> **whayong said:**
> Yes, I was able to log into enlightenment.  Infact, I've been using it for about a day now.  I can't remember now if those apps/libs I mentioned were skipped during the initial install.  I can tell you though that I don't have any of the enlightenment apps (picture viewer, media player, etc...).  

Ah, ok. Thats the normal behavior. The purpose here it's to get a stable core that can be used with other software more mature and that users may be more used (from other DEs). 
But you can get those apps (the ones you refer are quite stable theses days, i think) by editing the file **/etc/.easy_e17.conf** and remove the names of the wanted apps from --skip list. After that just do:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh --only=<eapp_name>
```
or for a safer option:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -i 
```
to make sure any lib and app it's compiled again.

> **whayong said:**
> 
A few more questions:

Has anyone got suspend/hibernate working on a laptop when the lid is closed?  It works when clicking the suspend/hibernate options but not when closing the lid.  I guess it's something I could live with.

Does anyone have any suggestions for firefox themes?  I guess it makes sense that GNOME apps aren't affected by the selected e17 theme.

How does one change the entrance theme?  There doesn't seem to be an option under configuration.

WICD works well with this by the way.

EDIT: I just re read the original post regarding gtk themes at startup.  This solved the whole firefox thing.  Is it possible to get suspend when laptop lid is closed the same way?  I'll give it a shot.

About the laptop lid i don't know any e17 method/application specific to do that (but maybe exist...) I use gnome-power-manager launched at enlightenment startup and gnome-power-preferences to tune behavior. Maybe it sounds a little overwhelming but it don't have too much gnome dependencies and works quite well.

About entrance... thats one of the set of buggy and sometimes keep long periods broken and not update. It miss some basic functionality like XDMCP and it's hard to configure under Ubuntu (i only made it work once and after a reboot it spots mysteriously... and read that people at geubuntu are getting problems to make net work when entrance is used...) 
But you can try it with the same process above and check how its development goes.

Thanks for give e17-cvs a try :)
Rui.

### Post by whayong on 2008-01-07
Thanks a lot for the help and thanks for the "how to."  Without it I would still be using gnome.  I did manage to get the lid working once "gnome-control-center" was running. ### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-07
> **whayong said:**
> Thanks a lot for the help and thanks for the "how to."  Without it I would still be using gnome.  I did manage to get the lid working once "gnome-control-center" was running.  So I guess what I would really need is gnome-power-manager.  I will do some more testing to confirm weather or not gnome-control-center at startup will do.

No problem, glad i can help :)

gnome-control-center, when runs from other DE other then gnome, launches several services from gnome, among them gnome-power-manager, gnome-settings-daemon and gnome-screensaver (if installed). 
Set any of those to run by hand make a better fine tunning of startup and lighter resources. 

Personally, i always run gnome-power-manager and gnome-settings-daemon, this late solves the gtk look without any need of a .gtkrc-2.0 file and with the extras better tools for tweak gtk look and automatic numlockx on at startup :)

---

Personally, i always run gnome-power-manager and gnome-settings-daemon, this late solves the gtk look without any need of a .gtkrc-2.0 file and with the extras better tools for tweak gtk look and automatic numlockx on at startup :)

### Post by airtonix on 2008-01-07
> **groggyboy said:**
> i had the same problem.  for anyone else with this error, this is what i did to solve it:

**1.** in the /var/lib/dpkg directory: i replaced "available", "diversions", "statoverride", and "status" with their "-old" counterparts
**2.a.** i deleted the following files: /var/e17-cvs.0.crash, /var/lib/dpkg/info/e17-cvs.conffiles, /var/lib/dpkg/info/e17-cvs.postinst, /var/lib/dpkg/info/e17-cvs.postrm and /var/lib/dpkg/info/e17-cvs.list
**2.b.** if you find any other files in /var/lib/dpkg and its subdirectories that contain the name "e17-cvs", delete them as well.
**3.** i ran "sudo apt-get update" followed by "sudo apt-get install -f"

this *should* fix things.


Had to cancel cvs download due : 

* script not allowing me to defeer process to later time
* download from cvs server hammered our bandwidth.
* flatemates was running an instance(Hmek) in WoW , cvs download gave her 2000ms ping.

so i killled the cvs download. and this method allowed me to continue using apt-get before this debacle.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-08
> **airtonix said:**
> Had to cancel cvs download due : 

* script not allowing me to defeer process to later time
* download from cvs server hammered our bandwidth.
* flatemates was running an instance(Hmek) in WoW , cvs download gave her 2000ms ping.

Hi,
sorry to hear about your problems. 
Of course downloading code requires some bandwidth... but not that much (it's just a few Megs). 
Maybe you hit a moment when server had problems or was under some kind of problems...


[COLOR="Blue"]What i want to advise/reinforce:[/COLOR]
**** DO NOT USE THOSE COMMANDS UNLESS AS LAST RESOURCE ****

**[COLOR="DarkRed"]Messing with those file can seriously broken your OS![/COLOR]**


If that kind of problems appears, user should 1st. try to finish installation at a later time and after that remove with **aptitude purge** if not want to use it, or simply prevent the code donload/compilation do a:
```
touch _skip_easy_e17_install
```
and then redo the apt-get command.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-14
Hi,
**[COLOR="DarkGreen"]i have updated the e17-cvs package and e17-themes.[/COLOR]**

It's important that e17-cvs users update to this new version. 
** DO NOT TRY TO UNINSTALL FIRST! **
This solves the issue that creates broken (non-uninstallable) e17-cvs when a remove was required. 

The packages now includes by default emprint and screenshot and removes scrot as a needed dependency.

I remove too the workaround for old ewl problem, since here it compiles fine at first (lets hope problem it's solved for good).

To install under hardy with a kde installation you may need to first install manually libgif-dev (did'nt test myself)


e17-theme includes updates for pager to work with latest versions of e17.

Don't forget that we are still without Cafelinux repos so you must use the alternative repos.

Thanks.

---

By the old method you mean the manual one on pdf? In that case you need to add emprint to app="e ..." line of easy_e17.sh. 
The best way it's still installing the e17-cvs deb. It takes care of all automatically. You don't need to delete or reinstall anything, just move any easy_e17.conf to ~/.easy_e17.conf and install the deb (repo or by gdebi)

emprint shouldn't be any problem... it just makes screenshot applet work (and make it independent of scrot app...)

Hi, glad to see you managed to solve your main issues :)

The reason because you don't had an Applications menu it's because enlightenment now implements menus as the standard freedesktop.org specifications, same as gnome and xfce, so if don't have one of those installed your menus can goes empty, or you have only kde that implements menus on a different way.

update-menu it's a nice way of create them. 
Another suggestions can be read here:
[http://wiki.enlightenment.org/index.php/E17_and_Efreet](http://wiki.enlightenment.org/index.php/E17_and_Efreet) (look for sample application.menu) 
or here:
[http://e17blog.tuxfamily.org/e17blog_en.php/post/2007/08/13/About-the-Applications-menu-files](http://e17blog.tuxfamily.org/e17blog_en.php/post/2007/08/13/About-the-Applications-menu-files)

You can get the defaults applications.menu from Ubuntu by installing gnome-menus from repos or if you don't what gnome you can just download the deb [from here]("http://packages.ubuntu.com/gutsy/gnome/gnome-menus") and extract the applications.menu to /etc/xdg/menus/applications.menu :)

To implement kde menus it should be enough to link:
/etc/xdg/menus/kde-applications.menu as /etc/xdg/menus/applications.menu (never tried but should work)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-17
> **tekknokrat said:**
> For me the gui way segfaults (on amd64) with both add applications / autostartentries

Yes, it needs absolute paths. it's weird it segfaults... i use 64b too but  i haven't that behavior.

About xcompmgr, check this thread here, for some tips:
[http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php/topic,894.0.html](http://cafelinux.org/forum/index.php/topic,894.0.html)

Or you can make a desktop file ~/.local/share/applications/xcompmgr.desktop 
> [Desktop Entry]
Hidden=false
Name[en_US]=Xcompmgr
NoDisplay=false
Exec=xcompmgr -cCfF -r7 -o.65 -l-10 -t-8 -D7
Type=Application
Version=1.0
Categories=Settings;
StartupNotify=false
GenericName=Xcompmgr
Name=Xcompmgr
Terminal=false
Comment=Xcompmgr
Comment[en_US]=Xcompmgr
GenericName[en_US]=Xcompmgr

good luck.
and from here it's easy to adapt

hi
well elbuntu it's not exactly a new project (i had even an impression that it's a dying one)

If you are interested in using an e17 distro based on precompiled packages you should check elive, gOS or geubuntu/openGEU and give an eye on Minte17 too (it's close to release i think...) 

But, please, note that you must remove completely this install first, before proceed to any of the others, since they conflict (different paths+different dates of code=disaster)

Just a note, openGEU/Geubuntu use the repod that elbuntu used and they have an geubuntu-desktop metapackage that can install all desktop without reinstall OS. 
The downsides... binaries became old quickly on this non-released software where changes happen on a daily bases (last time i check binaries were made on November 2007) and you can't fine tune all like on cvs compile.

Pick your choice :)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-17
> **neonl said:**
> Hello.

I changed the "border settings" of the OpenOffice.org main window (that one which lets you choose between text, spreadsheet, presentation, etc) to "no border". I did it because of that ugly effect of the yellow windows. The problem now is that I can't have the borders in any of the OO apps launched from that menu.

Does anyone know how to solve this? Thanks,
Rui *neonl*


Ok, workaround for this:
```
sudo cp /opt/e17/share/enlightenment/data/themes/default.edj /opt/e17/share/enlightenment/data/themes/default_ORIG.edj
```

```
sudo cp <your_actual_theme.edj> /opt/e17/share/enlightenment/data/themes/default.edj
enlightenment_remote -restart
```

e.g:
sudo cp ```
/opt/e17/share/enlightenment/data/themes/RustedEmerald.edj /opt/e17/share/enlightenment/data/themes/default.edj
```
gives a much coherent:
[ATTACH]56742[/ATTACH]

hth

No problem :)


About your issue. 
No, it was not necessary to uninstall before running from repo. e17-cvs was made specifically to take advantage of previous installations. It will only perform an update on if a previous installation it's detected. 
But if your previous installation did not finished successfully it will not be able to check that.

A simple solution it's just recompile (it will not download all again :)) e17 again.

Essentially what e17-cvs do it's ensure you have a sane compile environment, needed dependencies, conf files and script (patched). After package installed it's all done from command line using the internal patched script.

So basically what you need is:
1st. remove any manually made enlightenment.desktop (that are not made by easy_e17.sh script, so either you create that file by hand or downloaded from somewhere):
```
sudo rm /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
```
get the correct launcher:
```
sudo  ln -s /opt/e17/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
```
reinstall e17:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -i
```
(note that this is not the script you may have download, but the e17-cvs one)

updates after correct install are done by:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -u
```

Let me know if you still get problems after that.
Good luck.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-19
> **tekknokrat said:**
> hm, thats indeed strange. Because everything else works fine apart of bling ans sometime the themes wont change.

Could it be because i am using xserver from sid?
Some just in time debug possibilities?
When did you last time did an update of e17?

Maybe xserver on sid has some issue, don't know...
Or maybe some broken e config. Sometimes misterious segfaults disappear with a simple start froma fresh profile.
You can test these by doing with all sessions close, from a console (Ctrl+F1):
```
mv ~/.e ~/.e_BKUP
```
and then login.

I had experience crashs a couple of days ago. I got back to code from the previous day and update again after a few hours a nd problem disappeared, maybe you had the bad luck of get exactly the same bad time of cvs code... 
I usually update every morning (it's an habit like clean tooth and drink coffee :))


> **tekknokrat said:**
> I wasnt happy using the kde-applications nor the debian-applications.menu because they both showed no categories for me but a whole list of apps I didn't wanted in my menu.

When starting kde the menu always looked fine but not in e17 (symlink applications.menu -> kde-applications.menu). Perhaps some magic in kde itself? Someone else has other experience with kde menu?

Also too lazy to make my own menu I only copied the menu file from the dunnewind package (enlightenment-applications.menu) to /etc/xdg/menus and symlinked it to applications.menu.

Great! Thats a good solution.
I have done the same for OzOS with gnome menus (or if i keep gnome-menus it will bring a lot of gnome dependencies just to have applications.menu file...)

Another way of tweak menus it's using an external menu editor (by hand it's insane). 
I recommend alacarte, And if user don't gnome dependencies on his/her box, just take note of what it's installed with alacarte, personalize menu and remove alacarte and all that was installed with it. Simple and easy... 
just have to be done once, because any app installed after, got automatically on the menus.

### Post by kelvin spratt on 2008-01-20
Their is nothing wrong with Dunnewind that is why most Deb based Distros feed from it, Last week Dunnewind updated with no problem also if it does break there are instructions on the blog site on how to get it back up and running just like Rui does with OZ so that is not a problem really, I use both with no problems I started using Dunnewind  last June and yes their was a problem in Sept it was a major update but it just needed the correct input in the terminal to fix. and of course the theme makers to update as well.  Gutsy came out soon after and broke for many trying to do a distro upgrade that did not make it unreliable or a bit dodgy,  OZ, and Dunnewind, and Elive, flavours are the finest examples of what e17 can  achieve Mint,is not included as it is not yet released! Simple to install and very stable and do nothing but good for e17. Its very fast developing at the moment and is more stable day by day. Theme  breakage is common at the moment in all flavours after updates but the top themes on e17 stuff, and e17org, are usually updaded  in 24hrs so again not a problem, Long live e17.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-20
> **disturbedite said:**
> i'm trying to use that repo but when i try to install the e17 virtual package (to install the whole desktop environment) i get this:
```
e17:
 Depends: libevas0-loaders-all but it is not going to be installed
```
arrgggggggggggggghhhh, its cuz it still depends on libungif4...

disturbedite, i think you are asking to much... 
if you want to install over an unstable version of ubuntu + a special requirement, you should stick to the e17 most flexible possible method. Either plain manual install or this e17-cvs.

Lutin repos are great, one of the best and the reason why, as kelvin spratt said, most distros adopted... but pre-compiled binaries have necessarily limitations due to its nature (one can not expect that binary makers can build for every single variations, alpha and pre-releases). 
You require that someone do and release a repo for a alpha distro that requires libgif-dev instead of libungif-dev because of kde, another DE... Thats too much specification. 

I could attend you request because i simply make my deb when install configuration files, accept any of the -dev eventually installed, compilation was made on your machine, to your needs. 
If you require too a compilated binary, the repo should have a libgif-dev and a libungif-deb, (not to mention, several arch for those 2 specifications, or even several ubuntu flavors for those specification). 
Thats absolutely impracticable, either on time of repo maker as space for binaries.

Thanks Kelvin.
Thats what i tried from the beginning, doing a tool that could help people install e17 from cvs easily on a large set of distro or even minimal installs. A tool for easiness but flexible and power enough to work on those variety of environments.

I'm glad that it turns quite right and seems indeed to work very well on those conditions :)

My main obstacle has been, so far, get troubles for doing things that in bash are more elegant but strangely fail when run by automated dpkg (like the annoying problem of breakage of dpkg on remove...) 


About menus, thats not directly related with e17-cvs. CVS code don't creates a full menu... so i implemented on Oz OS with a copy of application.menu, but i like to keep that implementation on oz-desktop package and leave e17-cvs the more straight approach to e17 cvs code as possible. I will add a note on 1st post to how to install a copy of menu on installs that may not provide once. Thanks for bring the subject!

About penguins, well they are cute :) 
... but the idea it's to keep it simple as possible and avoid much extras, and as funny as they  are i doubt that any user will keep that module loaded much time. It's very distracting when one tries to concentrate on work and that flock of crazy creatures are behave wild everywhere in the screen :lol: 
(besides adds an extra amount of time and effort on old computers)

Of course, the problem of theme breakage it's common to any e17 version, but became more annoying on pre-binary installs for theme developers, because if they want to stick to a particular distro or repo they may have to maintain an old version of the theme until distro or repo update.

Thats the case the smartbouyathome refers. 
Themes specification are different since a week or two, but those repos are older than that. So either one have to maintain an updated version or develop themes specifically for that repo but that soon will became outdated (when Lutin update the repo)...

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-20
> **eFFeeMMe said:**
> Err sorry to bother again, but how do I actually get enlightenment to run on startup instead of GNOME?

No problem :)
Thats a gdm configuration. 
When you login it should ask you if you want to make Enlightenment the default session. Just say yes, and it's done.

You can do it by hand, editing the file ~/.dmrc
Change it to get the line:
```
Session=enlightenment
```
instead of gnome.

### Post by eFFeeMMe on 2008-01-20

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-20
> **eFFeeMMe said:**
> Thanks. I've tried E, but it's kinda screwed - it cannot change screen resolution(claiming a module was lacking), and on startup the first time it asked me to unload many modules. How can I fix these issues?

For the first question. 
Thats a known "issue". You must edit your /etc/X11/xorg.conf and delete any resolutions higher than the ones that your monitor really can stand (in fact it's more a feature, since it's preventing you change for resolutions listed by xorg.conf but dangerous to your monitor and that others DEs seems to be less conservative).

About the second i don't understand exactly what happened... do you mind recompile all e17:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -i
```
to make sure that ll modules are compiled again. 
Make an apt-get update && apt-get upgrade first to ensure you have the latest e17-cvs.
Compile all again it's annoying but maybe it cleans something lost from your previous abort installation.

Good luck.
Tell me how it goes.

(btw can you reload successfully the modules again?)

### Post by eFFeeMMe on 2008-01-20
Hm. Still the same problem, even after a succesful recompilation. It didn't complain about modules lacking(and asking me to go on or unload them), but it still says that Xrandr is missing(it isn't), and won't let me use a comfortable resolution(it treats my monitor as widescreen whereas it's not). ---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-20
> **eFFeeMMe said:**
> Hm. Still the same problem, even after a succesful recompilation. It didn't complain about modules lacking(and asking me to go on or unload them), but it still says that Xrandr is missing(it isn't), and won't let me use a comfortable resolution(it treats my monitor as widescreen whereas it's not). The login manager has the same resolution problem by the way.

So the module problems are solved.

The other one it's not an e17 problem. it's an incorrect resolution value on xorg.conf file (and thats why it affect too the login manager that it has nothing to do with e17)
Backup your actual xorg.conf:
```
sudo cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf_BAKUP
```

and after that do:
```
sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
```
choosing only resolutions you know your monitor are capable.

Restart Xserver (ctrl+Alt+Backspace) and see if its working.

### Post by boowill on 2008-01-21
> About your issue. 
No, it was not necessary to uninstall before running from repo. e17-cvs was made specifically to take advantage of previous installations. It will only perform an update on if a previous installation it's detected. 
But if your previous installation did not finished successfully it will not be able to check that.

A simple solution it's just recompile (it will not download all again ) e17 again.

Essentially what e17-cvs do it's ensure you have a sane compile environment, needed dependencies, conf files and script (patched). After package installed it's all done from command line using the internal patched script.

So basically what you need is:
1st. remove any manually made enlightenment.desktop (that are not made by easy_e17.sh script, so either you create that file by hand or downloaded from somewhere):
Code:
sudo rm /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
get the correct launcher:
Code:
sudo  ln -s /opt/e17/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
reinstall e17:
Code:
sudo easy_e17.sh -i
(note that this is not the script you may have download, but the e17-cvs one)

updates after correct install are done by:
Code:
sudo easy_e17.sh -u
Let me know if you still get problems after that.
Good luck.

Hello guys!
After a Week End of trying to fix my e17 (well I was skiing too... :) ), I was a little desesperate, but the light comes from Rui!
Well, in fact what Rui has written for my case (see above) was not working, I don't know why...
So I have done the following:
rm /root/e17_cvs
rm ~/e17_cvs (i don't why this was there !!!)
rm /usr/local/bin/e*
update and install e17_cvs
and lunch the script
and ... it works!!!!!!!!! :guitar:

so now I will play with e17 (it looks a bit slow, but my iMac is too...)

I would like to thank a lot Rui for his help and for his good-natured tolerance and give him a forum thanks for his very very great job! :):):):):)

---

### Post by disturbedite on 2008-01-23
as for the conflict between libgif-dev & libungif4-dev, it apparently has been resolved on hardy.
in order to make backports to gutsy easier, the devs/package maintainers have made the packages that depend on libgif & libungif interchangeable.  in other words, any package that depended on them before can accept libgif **OR** libungif.  so no more conflict.

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-23
> **neonl said:**
> Mine is terribly new (é a chatice de nunca parar quieto) I'm still doing experiments with distros and I got back to Ubuntu today. So it's a fresh install. I noticed that part of .po files failing to compile. The part of the theme menu is the most annoying.

(chatice ou pica ;))

maybe it just needs a fresh profile (it's always better check this first)
logout from any e17 session and from a console (Ctrl+Alt+F1) or another DE do a:
```
mv ~/.e ~/_e_BKUP
```
then login to e17 and try to change theme and check around...

I experienced a weird effect with shelfs set to auto-hide. they seem to not react to the mouse entering it unless i search for very specific points (probably module issues 'cause with some shelfs that don't happen...)

If that don't solve nothing, yes, you have to live with that goo'n'Ol' version :)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-27
> **neonl said:**
> Ok, it's simple. I'm downloading the 19/Jan/2008 source.

EDIT: compiling

Good. It should go well, now.


> **celyst said:**
> I've just installed e17-cvs by following the instructions on the first post, and everything 's great. Now how do I get entranced working? Is it included in the e17-cvs package? If not, where can I get it?

FYI, I am doing this install after doing a command-line install of Ubuntu 7.10. I've only installed xorg and e17-cvs so far, besides the base system.

Thats my favorite way to install too :)
About entance, i give up on that, cause it was always too unstable...
and it misses some features i find fundamental.
But you can try, of course (i found it unusually harder on ubuntu).

Just edit file: /etc/.easy_e17.conf and remove it from the --skip list and do:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh --only=entrance
```
to install it.
To configure you must do some google search. If i found some good tip or howto i point it to it.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-27
> **celyst said:**
> Thanks :). Ah, the joys of quick replies via a forum. I've been googling for nearly an hour for a method to install entranced. No wonder everyone starts a new thread when they have a problem.

> **celyst said:**
> Wait, it isn't working. I removed entrance from the .easy_e17.conf file and did "sudo easy_e17.sh --only=entrance", but it isn't installing anything.

sorry, i must had remove it from my patched version of easy_e17.sh.

You need to edit too:
/usr/bin/easy_e17.sh
and replace line 26
```
apps="e eclair evfs..."
```
by
```
apps="e entrance eclair evfs ..."
```

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-27
> **celyst said:**
> Got it to install, but I can't login with my account... I think this whole Enlightenment thing is way above my head right now. I'm gonna do a reinstall and go for kdm this time instead of entrance. Hopefully that turns out better, else I might just give up and go for Xubuntu...

But just for the sake of learning, how do I log in using Entrance?

I don't know... i always get weird issues like that with entrance. Once it allow me to entry but only once... if i logout, the second time failed.

There is no need to really reinstall. Just install kdm:
```
sudo apt-get install kdm
```
and log normally from there. It's much more easy.
If you prefer something light you have xdm or [slim]("http://slim.berlios.de").

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-27
> **celyst said:**
> 
I know kdm goes with KDE and gdm goes with GNOME, but in this case would it really matter which one I choose, since I'm using Enlightenment?

GDM it's bigger (16M) but relatively independent of gnome (it depends on gtk).

KDM it's lighter (1.7M) but really depends on KDE (it will require the installation of KDE-base with more 17M)

SLIM requires only 0.7M but may require more work/knowledge to set (never tried, seems to be  done with same configs as gdm)

It will depends on what other apps you prefer, if they are gtk/gnome apps or qt/kde ones.

HI, well i'm confused too... that path issue don't make any sense too me. It can be easily solved but i wonder what the problem is...

Do you mind to post the output of:
```
echo $PATH
```
and
```
slocate edje_decc
```
(just to make sure you have only one edje_decc in your disc)

btw, that should be an executable binary, so should not be need a ./ before the name of binary...

About the error message, thats normal, because since you moved to a path where you don't have permissions it fails when it tries to write the 1st uncompressed file, a png.

Till we trace down the problem you can do instead:
```
cd ~
/opt/e17/bin/edje_decc Desktop/Téléchargements/cthulhain_rui-0.5.edj
```
to uncompress to your home directory, or:
```
cd /home/will/Desktop/Téléchargements/
/opt/e17/bin/edje_decc cthulhain_rui-0.5.edj
to uncompress to /home/will/Desktop/Téléchargements/

```

Yes, absolutely. 
You still have, not only an old e17 installation as they cvs code. This last one just waste space, but the e17 installation can (and will) be a source of problems in updates.

The most problematic it's that you have the config part of install e17-cvs to launch enlightenment but you are running in fact from binaries on /usr/local/e17/bin. A mixed system. Thats it's even more unstable. It only runs now because they must be compiled from cvs on very close dates... but it's a pure luck it even runs!

What i don't get it is why e17-cvs failed to set the correct paths...

So, by now it's better correct those errors by hand.
Logout any e17 sessions and from a console (Ctrl+Alt+F1) or from a terminal on another DE do (if possible copy+paste) the following:  
```
sudo rm -rf /usr/local/cvs/e17
sudo mv /usr/local/e17 ~/e17_BKUP_TO_DELETE
cp /etc/environment /etc/.environment.bak
sudo sed -e "s/PATH=\"/PATH=\"\/opt\/e17\/bin\:/g"< /etc/.environment.bak > /etc/environment
sudo rm /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
sudo ln -s /opt/e17/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop /usr/share/xsessions/enlightenment.desktop
```
then logout the other DE or console (Ctrl+alt+F7)
And try to login to enlightenment.
If it works it's safe to rm -rf ~/e17_BKUP_TO_DELETE, if not work post that we try another approach.

good luck.

About the menus, it's always an issue on fresh installs.
Try:
```
update-desktop-database -q
```

If that fails or that package don't exist on Parsix, see my suggestions on this post here:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=4153905&highlight=menus#post4153905](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?p=4153905&highlight=menus#post4153905)

hope that helps.



EDIT: I add that link to 1st post, since it's a frequent issue for people installing on minimal bases or other distros.

Excellent! 
Don't worry about locate mention a file that don't exist anymore. slocate uses a files database (updated by a cron service), not a really search on disc. You can update manually with *sudo updatedb*.

About the edje files, start to change graphics on the edje. 
edje code i only try to read when i saw a theme that do something i wish to know... sometimes i simply mix the code from several themes to get some specific effect (my transparent effect on ibar of RustedEmerald it's an idea from an old elive theme and animation on menus of BlackEmerald it's from a theme on get-e.org). 
That part it's very flexible, one can mix easily as long as preserve file names.
Good luck, goos inspiration and if possible share your themes :)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-01-30
> **boowill said:**
> Hi Rui
thanks for the information about updatedb.
I have tried to modify the transparency of the shelf but i failed!
I have change the visible value to 1 in the default_ibar.edc file but without success. Do you remember which file I have to modify?
Thanks in advance... :popcorn:
Hi boowill, 
working on themes ;) good.

transparency are set on last numerical value of **color**, the 4th, on all default_ibar.edc file, ranging from 255 (opaque) to 1(or 0?, full transparent)
to get the efect of RustedEmerald, as an example:
```
color:    255 255 255 **128**;
```
so 128 set half transparency. The others are RGB values.

hth 

> **boowill said:**
> 
ps: should i rm the /root/e17_cvs folder ?
No, unless you really need the space.
That way any update will only download changes of that code and compile only apps with changes. If removed it will compile all again on each update. 
What i usually suggest it's move the e17_cvs to .e17_cvs ( and edit cvs_path="$HOME/e17_cvs" on easy_e17.sh or add the new path on .easy_e17.conf file)so it will not be visible and annoying... 
Pure cosmetic. As root it's not that important cause you should be root during much time. For Ubuntu users it's more important since that folder will be in users home, appearing on file manager and so on...

### Post by neonl on 2008-02-06
Hello.

Just to warn somebody who's going to fresh-install e17 that some problems with the CVS code (like the autohidding shelves that don't come up) are **NOT** solved yet...

The last stable CVS code still being the '20080119'.

If you have it installed and you want to return to the last sable code (19th Jan 2008 ) just do:
```
cd ~/.e17_cvs
sudo mv e17 e17bak # you can 'rm' it, but this way you'll keep a backup source of you current installation
sudo cvs -z3 -d :pserver:anonymous@anoncvs.enlightenment.org:/var/cvs/e co -D 20080119 e17 # this downloads from Enlightenment's CVS the 19/01/2008 code, but the '20080119' part can be substituted with any other in the format 'yyyymmdd'
sudo rm -rf /opt/e17 /rmp/easy_e17 # erase the binaries of current installation
sudo easy_e17.sh --skip-cvsupdate -i # compile the legacy code
```

Regards,
*neonl*

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-07
> **kelvin spratt said:**
> Rui
Just an update on Parsix most things are OK, The  Debian menus are not that brilliant but most of it is sort of there. I have one major problem its the Keyboard in Gnome it runs as Uk, as it should as I use a Uk keyboard, In e17 it defaults to US which is no good as the layout is wrong. I tried to use your script on the first page but nothing changed. I changed grub entry to Uk as default and in xserver but it only only works in Gnome not e17?

Hi again Kelvin.

Yes i agree with you plain Debian menu it's quite hard to get used...
Have you tried to copy a ubuntu applications.menu to your
/etc/xdg/menus/?
That's a much more simplified/organized menu.

About keyboard setting, contrary to Gnome, e17 don't have any tool specific to set keyboards internally (Gnome can set and use different keyboard layouts than the one used generic by the system).

You must set the one you have at /etc/X11/xorg.conf file.
For UK i think you need to have a
```
    Option "XkbLayout" "gb"
```

good luck

Yes thats right. 

Or put it more clearly, Xorg.conf should contain the config for the keyboard a user really have, (not sure about French code but should be the usual fr),independent of your choice of DE. 

Some DEs allow to overpass that choice (but thats not an option at e17... as far as i know). Should not be the other way, set that option on DEs to correct an incorrect xorg.conf setting...

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-08
> **neonl said:**
> Hello.

Just to warn somebody who's going to fresh-install e17 that some problems with the CVS code (like the autohidding shelves that don't come up) are **NOT** solved yet...

The last stable CVS code still being the '20080119'.

If you have it installed and you want to return to the last sable code (19th Jan 2008 ) just do:
```
cd ~/.e17_cvs
sudo mv e17 e17bak # you can 'rm' it, but this way you'll keep a backup source of you current installation
sudo cvs -z3 -d :pserver:anonymous@anoncvs.enlightenment.org:/var/cvs/e co -D 20080119 e17 # this downloads from Enlightenment's CVS the 19/01/2008 code, but the '20080119' part can be substituted with any other in the format 'yyyymmdd'
sudo rm -rf /opt/e17 /rmp/easy_e17 # erase the binaries of current installation
sudo easy_e17.sh --skip-cvsupdate -i # compile the legacy code
```

Regards,
*neonl*

Just an update for users that use the above tip (forced by the bug on auto-hide). 
For the time been, main e from that date work with updated libs...
So, after install the old version from 20080119, one can add e to --skip list (on /etc/.easy_e17.conf):
```
--skip=**e,**imlib2,edb,scrot,emotion,...
```
and then do a normal:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -u
```

---

Just to warn somebody who's going to fresh-install e17 that some problems with the CVS code (like the autohidding shelves that don't come up) are **NOT** solved yet...

The last stable CVS code still being the '20080119'.

If you have it installed and you want to return to the last sable code (19th Jan 2008 ) just do:
```
cd ~/.e17_cvs
sudo mv e17 e17bak # you can 'rm' it, but this way you'll keep a backup source of you current installation
sudo cvs -z3 -d :pserver:anonymous@anoncvs.enlightenment.org:/var/cvs/e co -D 20080119 e17 # this downloads from Enlightenment's CVS the 19/01/2008 code, but the '20080119' part can be substituted with any other in the format 'yyyymmdd'
sudo rm -rf /opt/e17 /rmp/easy_e17 # erase the binaries of current installation
sudo easy_e17.sh --skip-cvsupdate -i # compile the legacy code
```

Regards,
*neonl*

Just an update for users that use the above tip (forced by the bug on auto-hide). 
For the time been, main e from that date work with updated libs...
So, after install the old version from 20080119, one can add e to --skip list (on /etc/.easy_e17.conf):
```
--skip=**e,**imlib2,edb,scrot,emotion,...
```
and then do a normal:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh -u
```[/QUOTE]

This is what you should do. :)

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-09
> **sandysandy said:**
> for a fresh install of e17 what codes should be used.

regards

Just to clarify. There are 3 cases.
-User **don't use** auto-hide option of shelfs:
Install normally with the latest code.

-User **uses** auto-hide option of shelfs and want play safe:
use the tip on neonl post:
[post #626]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showpost.php?p=4282835&postcount=626")

-User **use** auto-hide option of shelfs but want to stay update:
after  done the procedure above for code of 2008/01/19
add e to --skip list and update.
That will maintain main e (the core) on that date but all rest updated.
There is always some chance that things break on some api change... meantime it's working normally.

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-12
Hi, 
> **kelvin spratt said:**
> Rui
Just a further update on OzParsix It settled down well and is very stable at the moment all installed software runs. Just a couple of niggles the Ubuntu menu some icons refuse to display on the application main menu, the individual application menus are OK. the icons issue it's, i think, due to the absence of a normalization for gtk themes... some icons work on gnome that fails on Xfce or other implementations of Freedesktop.org standardization.

You can try change the theme icons set on:
Configuration > configuration panel > Appearance > Icon Theme.
If you really want to use that one specifically you may need to manually add links of the existent png (usually something like gnome-*) to the standard format (system-* or x-*).
You can try several themes and check which name icon have on those that have an icon for that entry... (a boring process)

(edit) btw. i noted that some applications creates icons that make Applications menu slow on appearing (kompozer it's one of them)... i fail to see why.

Is there a way of creating *.edj* files with JPEG pictures (wallpapers) with the "use original file" option which isn't adding them one by one in the Wallpaper menu - a script or something...

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-12
> **neonl said:**
> Hi fellows :)

Is there a way of creating *.edj* files with JPEG pictures (wallpapers) with the "use original file" option which isn't adding them one by one in the Wallpaper menu - a script or something...

It would help a lot if there was one... I don't feel like adding 98 new pics manually :roll:

Oz or other?

-with Oz:
just select images on thunar and with right button of the mouse choose: 'Do Wallpaper'

-with others distros, i use e17setroot. It's on deprecated package e_utils.
Check 1st post, section Tips and Notes (3rd. tip).

just make a script (executable) with:
```
#!/bin/bash
e17setroot -s -n "$1"
```
and add the script to possible actions for jpeg, than you can select several and run the script on it 
:)

### Post by smartboyathome on 2008-02-12
Rui, would you happen to know how one would install entrance correctly? It installs to the /opt directory rather than to /etc and /usr. Also, how would I add proto apps to the script. I would like entrance_edit_gui, but the only way to get it is through the proto part of CVS.

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-15
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Rui, would you happen to know how one would install entrance correctly? It installs to the /opt directory rather than to /etc and /usr. Also, how would I add proto apps to the script. I would like entrance_edit_gui, but the only way to get it is through the proto part of CVS.

Hi, 
sorry my experiments with entrance all take to something not much reliable... 
Have you tried to make symlinks at /usr/ and /etc/ pointing the /opt ones?...

To add proto to script you need to edit your /usr/bin/easy_e17.sh (add entrance and entrance_edit_gui to line 26: apps=" e entrance ...")
and remove those from --skip line at /etc/.easy_e17.conf

You can then do:
sudo easy_e17.sh --only=entrance,entrance_edit_gui

About further configuration, check those links to see if some helps:
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=233688](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=233688)
[http://gopalarathnam.com/weblog/2006/11/20/getting-entrance-e17-display-manager-to-work-on-ubuntu.html](http://gopalarathnam.com/weblog/2006/11/20/getting-entrance-e17-display-manager-to-work-on-ubuntu.html)
good luck

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-02-21
Hi all,
just a quick update on auto-hide problems ( post 19/01/2008 )

Apparently the problem only happen to shelf set to auto-hide with the same direction of virtual desktops... 

So if you have several virtual desktop vertical aligned but auto-hide shelves are all horizontal, or vice-versa, it will work normally (as expected).

Maybe this helps till problem will be solved.
Rui


***EDIT:***
btw, here it's the buzilla entry for this bug:
[http://bugzilla.enlightenment.org/show_bug.cgi?id=394](http://bugzilla.enlightenment.org/show_bug.cgi?id=394)

---

### Post by earlycj5 on 2008-02-29
> **eFFeeMMe said:**
> Hm. Still the same problem, even after a succesful recompilation. It didn't complain about modules lacking(and asking me to go on or unload them), but it still says that Xrandr is missing(it isn't), and won't let me use a comfortable resolution(it treats my monitor as widescreen whereas it's not). The login manager has the same resolution problem by the way.

I know I'm late to the party but I was having the same issue with xrandr.

I need it to hook my laptop up to external projectors and it wouldn't work.

The whole message says something about xrandr being missing OR it wasn't present when e17 was compiled.  Remember with OzOS the e17 is compiled from the e17 cvs on your computer.  It's not a precompiled package like Gnome is.

Unless you have the libxrander-dev package installed it will not work.  I went through this just now.  xrander was there, worked in Gnome but not e17.  Installed the development package for xrander, ran "easy_e17.sh -i" and once it was done, restarted e17.  Voila, I can adjust my resolution using the e17 tools.

Hope this helps someone else in the future.

---

Unless you have the libxrander-dev package installed it will not work.  I went through this just now.  xrander was there, worked in Gnome but not e17. ### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-03-02
> **Rui Pais said:**
> I don't have any issue and i have my e17 compiled only with xrandr and the generic libxrandr2 (no libxrandr-dev).


Ooops... Not at all!!

I've checked again with more attention and yes you are right, **ecore** did NEED TO BE BUILD with libxrandr-dev!!

Sorry by let that one out of dependencies.
A new corrected package its available now.

*EDIT:*
Of course, users with an e17 already installed should do, after update:
```
sudo easy_e17.sh --only=ecore
```

### Post by smartboyathome on 2008-03-03
Rui, I am trying to install ecomorph, it won't compile, even after I run PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$PKG_CONFIG_PATH:/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig and then compile. Help? Thanks. :)

---

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-03-04
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Rui, I am trying to install ecomorph, it won't compile, even after I run PKG_CONFIG_PATH=$PKG_CONFIG_PATH:/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig and then compile. Help? Thanks. :)

Hi smartboyathome.
Hi don't think you need to tune PKG_CONFIG_PATH... as long as 
```
echo $PKG_CONFIG_PATH 
``` outputs
```
/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig
```
should work.

To keep compatibility with e installed on /opt/e17, you must run autogen (or ./configure) with:
```
./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/e17
```

hth

### Post by smartboyathome on 2008-03-04

Hi don't think you need to tune PKG_CONFIG_PATH... as long as 
```
echo $PKG_CONFIG_PATH 
``` outputs
```
/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig
```
should work.

To keep compatibility with e installed on /opt/e17, you must run autogen (or ./configure) with:
```
./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/e17
```

Ok, i checked here, and i realized i added PKG_CONFIG_PATH once (for testing exalt) to environment. Thats should be whats failing...

Add:
export ```
PKG_CONFIG_PATH="/opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig:$PKG_CONFIG_PATH"
```
to your .bashrc file, close all open terminals, and try again
```
./autogen.sh --prefix=/opt/e17
```
good luck, smartboyathome.



Edit:
note that PKG_CONFIG path should be: /opt/e17/lib/pkgconfig and that directory should contain (on a sane e17 install) all .pc files referred on your error output as missing.

```
sudo easy_e17.sh -i 
```
again and see if it goes over the error.
Btw, did it give any error messages when installed dependencies?

Maybe trying to get the old list of dependencies may help:```

sudo apt-get install libtool libltdl3-dev automake1.9 autotools-dev libpopt-dev libcurl3-dev libx11-dev x11proto-xext-dev libbz2-dev libid3tag0-dev libpng12-dev libtiff4-dev libungif4-dev libjpeg62-dev libssl-dev libfreetype6-dev bison flex xlibs-dev gettext libimlib2-dev libxml2-dev libxcursor-dev libgtk1.2-dev autoconf pkg-config libpng3-dev libxine-dev libxkbfile-dev libsqlite3-dev giblib-dev libxmu-dev libxdamage-dev libxcomposite-dev libtag1-dev libtagc0-dev giblib-dev libasound2-dev
```

and after that try: 
```
sudo rm -rf /tmp/easy_e17
sudo easy_e17.sh -i
```

If that stills fail, i'll have to look deeper for other possible causes (tomorrow).

Good luck

### Post by Rui Pais on 2008-03-06
> **smartboyathome said:**
> Rui, your reply worked, but now when I try to get the second thing compiled autoconf breaks, and says the same thing as obdata. I don't think it is a dapper drake problem.

Hi smartboyathome.
By the second thing you mean compiz or ecompconfig-python?

That kind of error it's usually from automake/autoconf conflict versions (if my memory from my gentoo days serves me well, thats what it usually happen when one wants to compile with incorrect compile tools versions...) 
That means that the versions of one of those tools it's incorrect for the makefile on the code :( (again, i'm not 100% sure of that)

What i suspect it's that e17 code may have something conflicting with dapper versions of autoconf/automake (this last it's the 1.9, but who knows...)

In the case of [ecomorph]("http://code.google.com/p/itask-module/wiki/Stuff") i compiled e, compiz and libecompconfig without problems but i got errors on ecompconfig-python...

sudo rm -rf /opt/e17 ~/.e17_cvs /tmp/easy_e17

```
